markdown.markdown:
These eight packages were used in the data cleaning, wrangling, and visualization steps of the report: ```{r setup, message = FALSE} library(tidyverse) library(readr) library(lubridate) library(plotly) library(knitr) library(tidyr) library(DT) library(htmltools) ``` ```{r setup1, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE} opts_chunk$set( warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, eval=TRUE, ### SET THIS TO TRUE TO RUN ALL CODE ### echo = TRUE, cache=FALSE, include=TRUE, fig.width = 7, fig.align = 'center', fig.asp = 0.618, out.width = "700px") ``` The US overdose deaths data were acquired from the [National Vital Statistics System (NVSS)](https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nvss/index.htm) and downloaded as a `.csv` file. For every month between January 2015 to October 2025, this dataset captures the number of new reported overdose deaths attributed to specific drugs classes, at the state and national level. More specific details about the data collection process are discussed in the **"About the Data" \[LINK\]** tab. ```{r, echo = FALSE} drug_deaths <- read.csv("VSRR_Provisional_Drug_Overdose_Death_Counts.csv") drug_deaths_preview <- drug_deaths[c(74231, 30021), ] %>% as.tibble() knitr::kable(head(drug_deaths_preview, 2), caption = "Preview of Raw NVSS Dataset") ## CHOOSE A BETTER "HEAD" ``` ```{r, echo = FALSE, output = FALSE} drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% select(c(1:6, 9)) %>% mutate(Deaths = as.numeric(parse_number(Data.Value)), Date = my(paste(Month, Year, sep = ",")), State.Abb = State, State = State.Name) %>% relocate(Month, .before = Year) %>% relocate(Date, .after = Year) %>% relocate(Deaths, .after = Indicator) %>% relocate(State.Abb, .before = State) drug_deaths$Data.Value <- NULL drug_deaths$State.Name <- NULL # Remove "Period" column because all values are the same # table(drug_deaths$Period) drug_deaths$Period <- NULL # Look at "State.Name": Notice District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, United States, and New York City table(drug_deaths$State) %>% as.data.frame() # The total death count for the United States does not include deaths occurring in Puerto Rico. drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% filter(!State == "Puerto Rico") # Combine New York City and New York together: new_york <- drug_deaths %>% filter(State == "New York" | State == "New York City") new_york$State <- NULL new_york <- new_york %>% pivot_wider(names_from = State.Abb, values_from = Deaths) %>% mutate(Deaths = NY + YC) new_york <- new_york %>% select(Month, Year, Date, Indicator, Deaths) %>% mutate(State.Abb = rep("NY", dim(new_york)[1]), State = rep("New York", dim(new_york)[1])) %>% relocate(c(State.Abb, State), .before = Month) # Add in "New York" (state) values back to dataset drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% filter(State != "New York") %>% filter(State != "New York City") drug_deaths <- rbind(drug_deaths, new_york) ``` To begin clean the data, the `Period`, `Percent.Pending.Investigation`, `Footnote`, `Foot.Symbol`, and `Predicted.Values` columns were removed. A `Date` variable was added to concatenate the month and year data. After inspecting the `State` categories, it was decided to filter out *Puerto Rico* data. Furthermore, data for *New York City* and *New York* were combined into one category, *New York*. These decisions are covered in more detail under the **"About the Data" \[LINK\]** tab. As previously mentioned, this dataset captures the monthly incident overdose deaths caused by many different classes of drugs, which are captured under the `Indicator` variable. ```{r preview_indicators, echo = FALSE} indicators <- unique(drug_deaths$Indicator) %>% as.data.frame() colnames(indicators) <- c("Causes of Overdose Death") knitr::kable(indicators, caption = "Causes of Overdose Deaths captured in the NVSS Dataset") ``` Given the goals of this this analysis, the `Indicators` column was filtered to five categories that captured incident numbers of overdose deaths related to different opioid classes: 1. *Heroin (T40.1)* 2. *Natural & semi-synthetic opioids (T40.2)* 3. *Methadone (T40.3)* 4. *Synthetic opioids, excl. methadone (T40.4)* 5. *Number of Drug Overdose Deaths* \[comparison group\] More information on the classifications of opioid-related deaths (using ICD-10 codes, eg. *T40.1*) and background on these opioids can be found in the **"About the Data" \[LINK\]** tab. Of important note, category #4, *Synthetic opioids, excl. methadone,* classifies overdose deaths attributed to fentanyl and its derivatives. This is the class of opioids tied to the "third wave" of the US opioid overdose crisis and that will be of particular interest to this analysis. ```{r indicators, echo = FALSE} # Filter to indicators of interest drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% filter(Indicator == "Heroin (T40.1)" | Indicator == "Methadone (T40.3)" | Indicator == "Number of Drug Overdose Deaths" | Indicator == "Natural & semi-synthetic opioids (T40.2)" | Indicator == "Synthetic opioids, excl. methadone (T40.4)") %>% mutate(Indicator = case_when( Indicator == "Heroin (T40.1)" ~ "Heroin", Indicator == "Methadone (T40.3)" ~ "Methadone", Indicator == "Number of Drug Overdose Deaths" ~ "Drug.Overdose", Indicator == "Natural & semi-synthetic opioids (T40.2)" ~ "Natural.Synthetic", TRUE ~ "Synthetic" )) drug_deaths$Indicator <- with(drug_deaths, factor(Indicator, levels = c("Heroin", "Natural.Synthetic", "Methadone", "Synthetic", "Drug.Overdose"))) drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Indicator, values_from = Deaths) ``` Next, for the state-level analysis to be normalized by population, US yearly census data was added to the NVSS dataset. This population data were acquired from United States Census Bureau ([2010-2020](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/popest/technical-documentation/research/evaluation-estimates/2020-evaluation-estimates/2010s-state-total.html), [2020-2025](https://www.census.gov/data/tables/time-series/demo/popest/2020s-state-total.html)) and downloaded as `.xlsx` files. After cleaning the population data, it was attatched to the NVSS table. Two additional columns, `drugdeathper100k` and `syntheticdeathper100k` were created to capture the number of deaths per 100,000 in the population from *all drug overdose deaths* and *synthetic opioids*, respectively. ```{r, message = FALSE, echo = FALSE} # Population of 2010s pop_2010s <- readxl::read_xlsx("pop_2010.xlsx") pop_2010s <- pop_2010s %>% select(-2) colnames(pop_2010s) <- c("State", seq(2010, 2020, 1)) pop_2010s <- pop_2010s[-c(1:3, 5:8, 60:67),] pop_2010s$State <- gsub("\\.", "", pop_2010s$State) pop_2010s <- pop_2010s %>% mutate(across(`2010`:`2020`, as.numeric)) %>% pivot_longer(cols = `2010`:`2020`, names_to = "Year", values_to = "Population") # Population of 2020s pop_2020s <- readxl::read_xlsx("pop_2020.xlsx") pop_2020s <- pop_2020s %>% select(-c(2:3)) colnames(pop_2020s) <- c("State", seq(2021, 2025, 1)) pop_2020s <- pop_2020s[-c(1:3, 5:8, 60:67),] pop_2020s$State <- gsub("\\.", "", pop_2020s$State) pop_2020s <- pop_2020s %>% mutate(across(`2021`:`2025`, as.numeric)) %>% pivot_longer(cols = `2021`:`2025`, names_to = "Year", values_to = "Population") pop <- rbind(pop_2010s, pop_2020s) %>% mutate(Year = as.numeric(Year)) # Adding Population to drug_deaths drug_deaths <- left_join(drug_deaths, pop, by = c("State", "Year")) # Creating "drugdeathper100k" drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% mutate(drugdeathper100k = round((Drug.Overdose/Population)*100000)) %>% mutate(syntheticdeathper100k = round((Synthetic/Population)*100000)) ``` Finally, the NVSS data set was filtered into two separate data sets based on the `State` variable, to separate US vs state-level data. In the data set for US-level data, the cumulative number of reported overdose deaths attributed to each opioid (starting in 2015) was calculated for each month. ```{r, echo = FALSE} # Split into National vs State-Level Datasets drug_deaths_nat <- drug_deaths %>% filter(State.Abb == "US") drug_deaths_state <- drug_deaths %>% filter(State.Abb != "US") ## Cumulative Deaths (national) drug_deaths_nat <- drug_deaths_nat %>% mutate(across(c(6:10), ~ cumsum(.x), .names = "Cumul.{.col}")) ``` ```{r, echo=FALSE} as.data.frame(head(drug_deaths_nat, 3)) %>% knitr::kable(caption = "Preview of Cleaned NVSS Dataset for US Data") as.data.frame(drug_deaths_state[c(6126, 6576, 2074), ]) %>% knitr::kable(caption = "Preview of Cleaned NVSS Dataset for State-Level Data") ``` Tools from the `plotly` and `DT` packages were used to visualize these data in the following results section. old cumulative calculations code: ```{r} drug_deaths_nat %>% plot_ly(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Drug.Overdose, type = "bar", marker = list(color = "black"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Drug Overdose\nDeaths:", Cumul.Drug.Overdose, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", textposition = "none", name = "All Drugs") %>% ## SYNETHIC add_trace(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Synthetic, type = "scatter", mode = "lines", line = list(color = "lightblue"), marker = list(color = "lightblue"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Synthetic Opioid Overdose\nDeaths: ", Cumul.Synthetic, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", name = "Synthetic Opioids") %>% ## NATURAL AND SEMI-SYNTHETIC add_trace(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Natural.Synthetic, type = "scatter", mode = "lines", line = list(color = "pink"), marker = list(color = "pink"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Natural & Semi-Synthetic Overdose\nDeaths: ", Cumul.Natural.Synthetic, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", name = "Natural & Semi-Synthetic\nOpioids") %>% ## HEROIN add_trace(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Heroin, type = "scatter", mode = "lines", line = list(color = "lightgreen"), marker = list(color = "lightgreen"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Heroin Overdose\nDeaths: ", Cumul.Heroin, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", name = "Heroin") %>% ## METHADONE add_trace(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Methadone, type = "scatter", mode = "lines", line = list(color = "orange"), marker = list(color = "orange"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Methadone Overdose\nDeaths: ", Cumul.Methadone, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", name = "Methadone") %>% layout(hovermode = "x unified", hoverlabel = list( align = "left", bgcolor = "white", font = list(size = 10)), xaxis = list(hoverformat = " %B %Y "), yaxis = list(title = "Cumulative Deaths"), title = "Figure 1b: Cumulative Reported Drug Overdose Deaths\nin the United States, from 2015-2025", margin = list(l = 40, r = 40, t = 100, b = 60)) ```

## METHODS: These eight packages were used in the data cleaning, wrangling, and visualization steps of the report: ```{r setup, message = FALSE} library(tidyverse) library(readr) library(lubridate) library(plotly) library(knitr) library(tidyr) library(DT) library(htmltools) ``` ```{r setup1, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE} opts_chunk$set( warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, eval=TRUE, ### SET THIS TO TRUE TO RUN ALL CODE ### echo = TRUE, cache=FALSE, include=TRUE, fig.width = 7, fig.align = 'center', fig.asp = 0.618, out.width = "700px") ``` The US overdose deaths data were acquired from the [National Vital Statistics System (NVSS)](https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nvss/index.htm) and downloaded as a `.csv` file. For every month between January 2015 to October 2025, this dataset captures the number of new reported overdose deaths attributed to specific drugs classes, at the state and national level. More specific details about the data collection process are discussed in the **"About the Data" \[LINK\]** tab. ```{r, echo = FALSE} drug_deaths <- read.csv("VSRR_Provisional_Drug_Overdose_Death_Counts.csv") drug_deaths_preview <- drug_deaths[c(74231, 30021), ] %>% as.tibble() knitr::kable(head(drug_deaths_preview, 2), caption = "Preview of Raw NVSS Dataset") ## CHOOSE A BETTER "HEAD" ``` ```{r, echo = FALSE, output = FALSE} drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% select(c(1:6, 9)) %>% mutate(Deaths = as.numeric(parse_number(Data.Value)), Date = my(paste(Month, Year, sep = ",")), State.Abb = State, State = State.Name) %>% relocate(Month, .before = Year) %>% relocate(Date, .after = Year) %>% relocate(Deaths, .after = Indicator) %>% relocate(State.Abb, .before = State) drug_deaths$Data.Value <- NULL drug_deaths$State.Name <- NULL # Remove "Period" column because all values are the same # table(drug_deaths$Period) drug_deaths$Period <- NULL # Look at "State.Name": Notice District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, United States, and New York City table(drug_deaths$State) %>% as.data.frame() # The total death count for the United States does not include deaths occurring in Puerto Rico. drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% filter(!State == "Puerto Rico") # Combine New York City and New York together: new_york <- drug_deaths %>% filter(State == "New York" | State == "New York City") new_york$State <- NULL new_york <- new_york %>% pivot_wider(names_from = State.Abb, values_from = Deaths) %>% mutate(Deaths = NY + YC) new_york <- new_york %>% select(Month, Year, Date, Indicator, Deaths) %>% mutate(State.Abb = rep("NY", dim(new_york)[1]), State = rep("New York", dim(new_york)[1])) %>% relocate(c(State.Abb, State), .before = Month) # Add in "New York" (state) values back to dataset drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% filter(State != "New York") %>% filter(State != "New York City") drug_deaths <- rbind(drug_deaths, new_york) ``` To begin clean the data, the `Period`, `Percent.Pending.Investigation`, `Footnote`, `Foot.Symbol`, and `Predicted.Values` columns were removed. A `Date` variable was added to concatenate the month and year data. After inspecting the `State` categories, it was decided to filter out *Puerto Rico* data. Furthermore, data for *New York City* and *New York* were combined into one category, *New York*. These decisions are covered in more detail under the **"About the Data" \[LINK\]** tab. As previously mentioned, this dataset captures the monthly incident overdose deaths caused by many different classes of drugs, which are captured under the `Indicator` variable. ```{r preview_indicators, echo = FALSE} indicators <- unique(drug_deaths$Indicator) %>% as.data.frame() colnames(indicators) <- c("Causes of Overdose Death") knitr::kable(indicators, caption = "Causes of Overdose Deaths captured in the NVSS Dataset") ``` Given the goals of this this analysis, the `Indicators` column was filtered to five categories that captured incident numbers of overdose deaths related to different opioid classes: 1. *Heroin (T40.1)* 2. *Natural & semi-synthetic opioids (T40.2)* 3. *Methadone (T40.3)* 4. *Synthetic opioids, excl. methadone (T40.4)* 5. *Number of Drug Overdose Deaths* \[comparison group\] More information on the classifications of opioid-related deaths (using ICD-10 codes, eg. *T40.1*) and background on these opioids can be found in the **"About the Data" \[LINK\]** tab. Of important note, category #4, *Synthetic opioids, excl. methadone,* classifies overdose deaths attributed to fentanyl and its derivatives. This is the class of opioids tied to the "third wave" of the US opioid overdose crisis and that will be of particular interest to this analysis. ```{r indicators, echo = FALSE} # Filter to indicators of interest drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% filter(Indicator == "Heroin (T40.1)" | Indicator == "Methadone (T40.3)" | Indicator == "Number of Drug Overdose Deaths" | Indicator == "Natural & semi-synthetic opioids (T40.2)" | Indicator == "Synthetic opioids, excl. methadone (T40.4)") %>% mutate(Indicator = case_when( Indicator == "Heroin (T40.1)" ~ "Heroin", Indicator == "Methadone (T40.3)" ~ "Methadone", Indicator == "Number of Drug Overdose Deaths" ~ "Drug.Overdose", Indicator == "Natural & semi-synthetic opioids (T40.2)" ~ "Natural.Synthetic", TRUE ~ "Synthetic" )) drug_deaths$Indicator <- with(drug_deaths, factor(Indicator, levels = c("Heroin", "Natural.Synthetic", "Methadone", "Synthetic", "Drug.Overdose"))) drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Indicator, values_from = Deaths) ``` Next, for the state-level analysis to be normalized by population, US yearly census data was added to the NVSS dataset. This population data were acquired from United States Census Bureau ([2010-2020](https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/popest/technical-documentation/research/evaluation-estimates/2020-evaluation-estimates/2010s-state-total.html), [2020-2025](https://www.census.gov/data/tables/time-series/demo/popest/2020s-state-total.html)) and downloaded as `.xlsx` files. After cleaning the population data, it was attatched to the NVSS table. Two additional columns, `drugdeathper100k` and `syntheticdeathper100k` were created to capture the number of deaths per 100,000 in the population from *all drug overdose deaths* and *synthetic opioids*, respectively. ```{r, message = FALSE, echo = FALSE} # Population of 2010s pop_2010s <- readxl::read_xlsx("pop_2010.xlsx") pop_2010s <- pop_2010s %>% select(-2) colnames(pop_2010s) <- c("State", seq(2010, 2020, 1)) pop_2010s <- pop_2010s[-c(1:3, 5:8, 60:67),] pop_2010s$State <- gsub("\\.", "", pop_2010s$State) pop_2010s <- pop_2010s %>% mutate(across(`2010`:`2020`, as.numeric)) %>% pivot_longer(cols = `2010`:`2020`, names_to = "Year", values_to = "Population") # Population of 2020s pop_2020s <- readxl::read_xlsx("pop_2020.xlsx") pop_2020s <- pop_2020s %>% select(-c(2:3)) colnames(pop_2020s) <- c("State", seq(2021, 2025, 1)) pop_2020s <- pop_2020s[-c(1:3, 5:8, 60:67),] pop_2020s$State <- gsub("\\.", "", pop_2020s$State) pop_2020s <- pop_2020s %>% mutate(across(`2021`:`2025`, as.numeric)) %>% pivot_longer(cols = `2021`:`2025`, names_to = "Year", values_to = "Population") pop <- rbind(pop_2010s, pop_2020s) %>% mutate(Year = as.numeric(Year)) # Adding Population to drug_deaths drug_deaths <- left_join(drug_deaths, pop, by = c("State", "Year")) # Creating "drugdeathper100k" drug_deaths <- drug_deaths %>% mutate(drugdeathper100k = round((Drug.Overdose/Population)*100000)) %>% mutate(syntheticdeathper100k = round((Synthetic/Population)*100000)) ``` Finally, the NVSS data set was filtered into two separate data sets based on the `State` variable, to separate US vs state-level data. In the data set for US-level data, the cumulative number of reported overdose deaths attributed to each opioid (starting in 2015) was calculated for each month. ```{r, echo = FALSE} # Split into National vs State-Level Datasets drug_deaths_nat <- drug_deaths %>% filter(State.Abb == "US") drug_deaths_state <- drug_deaths %>% filter(State.Abb != "US") ## Cumulative Deaths (national) drug_deaths_nat <- drug_deaths_nat %>% mutate(across(c(6:10), ~ cumsum(.x), .names = "Cumul.{.col}")) ``` ```{r, echo=FALSE} as.data.frame(head(drug_deaths_nat, 3)) %>% knitr::kable(caption = "Preview of Cleaned NVSS Dataset for US Data") as.data.frame(drug_deaths_state[c(6126, 6576, 2074), ]) %>% knitr::kable(caption = "Preview of Cleaned NVSS Dataset for State-Level Data") ``` Tools from the `plotly` and `DT` packages were used to visualize these data in the following results section. old cumulative calculations code: ```{r} drug_deaths_nat %>% plot_ly(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Drug.Overdose, type = "bar", marker = list(color = "black"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Drug Overdose\nDeaths:", Cumul.Drug.Overdose, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", textposition = "none", name = "All Drugs") %>% ## SYNETHIC add_trace(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Synthetic, type = "scatter", mode = "lines", line = list(color = "lightblue"), marker = list(color = "lightblue"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Synthetic Opioid Overdose\nDeaths: ", Cumul.Synthetic, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", name = "Synthetic Opioids") %>% ## NATURAL AND SEMI-SYNTHETIC add_trace(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Natural.Synthetic, type = "scatter", mode = "lines", line = list(color = "pink"), marker = list(color = "pink"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Natural & Semi-Synthetic Overdose\nDeaths: ", Cumul.Natural.Synthetic, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", name = "Natural & Semi-Synthetic\nOpioids") %>% ## HEROIN add_trace(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Heroin, type = "scatter", mode = "lines", line = list(color = "lightgreen"), marker = list(color = "lightgreen"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Heroin Overdose\nDeaths: ", Cumul.Heroin, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", name = "Heroin") %>% ## METHADONE add_trace(x = ~Date, y = ~Cumul.Methadone, type = "scatter", mode = "lines", line = list(color = "orange"), marker = list(color = "orange"), text = with(drug_deaths_nat, paste( "Methadone Overdose\nDeaths: ", Cumul.Methadone, "
")), hovertemplate = "%{text}", name = "Methadone") %>% layout(hovermode = "x unified", hoverlabel = list( align = "left", bgcolor = "white", font = list(size = 10)), xaxis = list(hoverformat = " %B %Y "), yaxis = list(title = "Cumulative Deaths"), title = "Figure 1b: Cumulative Reported Drug Overdose Deaths\nin the United States, from 2015-2025", margin = list(l = 40, r = 40, t = 100, b = 60)) ```

language.appendix-attribution-cite-as: For attribution, please cite this work as:
